title: Compose Development, Overrides, Profiles, Watch, and Test Workflows · Release Workshop
summary: A platform pairing session must produce the first working development and test Compose workflow matrix for compose development, overrides, profiles, watch, and test workflows, with predictions recorded before each simulated build or lifecycle change. Planned artifact: Build a complete first version of development and test Compose workflow matrix while every earlier competency remains active. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evid…
objectives: Merge base, development, test, and local Compose files while predicting mapping, sequence, reset, replace, and path resolution behavior.
Use profiles and explicit service targeting for optional tools without silently omitting required dependencies.
Choose sync, rebuild, restart, sync-and-restart, initial sync, and ignore behavior from code, dependency, config, and platform changes.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# docker-basics cumulative container delivery portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never connect to a Docker daemon, mount a socket, or execute learner commands.
workspace: docker
version-baseline:
  engine: 29.6.1
  compose: 5.3.1
  buildx: 0.35.0
  buildkit: 0.31.1
  dockerfile-frontend: 1.25
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Compose Files Merge Reset
steps.instruction:
workshop case — A workshop override bind-mounts production paths, watch rebuilds on generated files, and dev and test profiles silently change dependencies. Render every file combination and isolate disposable state. Competency probe: Merge base, development, test, and local Compose files while predicting mapping, sequence, reset, replace, and path resolution behavior.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Workshop Docker Compose Files Merge Reset.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for merge base, development, test, and local compose files while predicting mapping, sequence, reset, replace, and path resolution behavior. steps.title: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Compose Config Dry Run
steps.instruction:
Append the Docker evidence item headed "  # Evidence: docker-compose-config-dry-run-edq2cm". Tailor its compose.dev.yaml change to the current stakeholder case, retain a deterministic review command, reject one concrete failure, and record a repair. The browser analyzes text only; run Docker commands later in an authorized disposable transfer environment.

Evidence target: Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Compose Config Dry Run.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Docker Compose Config Dry Run into observable performance evidence. For Extend the cumulative artifact · Workshop Docker Compose Config Dry Run, keep Docker Compose Config Dry Run connected to mechanically reviewable Docker artifact and transfer evidence.

content.code:
# Evidence: docker-compose-config-dry-run-edq2cm
  - competency: docker-compose-config-dry-run
    artifact: compose.dev.yaml
    decision: compose config dry run
    scenario-variant: 5
    environment: air-gapped release worker with an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: invalidate one cache input without changing application source
    retained-proof: rendered Compose model and dependency health transition
    case-key: edq2cm
    change: |
      services:
      api:
      develop:
      watch:
      - action: sync
      path: ./src
      profiles: [dev]
      test:
      profiles: [test]
    verify: docker compose -f compose.yaml -f compose.dev.yaml config --profiles
    failure: Reject production bind mounts, hidden override precedence, watch-triggered dependency churn, or a profile that changes the core dependency contract.
    repair: Render every file combination, scope development-only state, select sync versus rebuild deliberately, and prove test teardown is isolated.
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax
content.text: The structural check is only one gate. The following changed-case decision and explanation must also hold.
